headline: Charles Schwab Foundation Launches Schwab Moneywise Momentum Grants as Part of a $20 Million Multi-Year Commitment to Financial Education
summary: WESTLAKE, Texas, May 27, 2026--Charles Schwab Foundation announced an open call for nonprofit organizations to apply for Schwab Moneywise Momentum Grants, a new annual grant program designed to support fresh thinking and creative solutions that help people build financial knowledge and confidence to make informed money decisions.

content.title: Charles Schwab Foundation Launches Schwab Moneywise Momentum Grants as Part of a $20 Million Multi-Year Commitment to Financial Education
content.summary: WESTLAKE, Texas, May 27, 2026--Charles Schwab Foundation announced an open call for nonprofit organizations to apply for Schwab Moneywise Momentum Grants, a new annual grant program designed to support fresh thinking and creative solutions that help people build financial knowledge and confidence to make informed money decisions.

thumbnail.caption: Charles Schwab Foundation announced an open call for nonprofit organizations to apply for Schwab Moneywise Momentum Grants, a new annual grant program designed to support fresh thinking and creative solutions that help people build financial knowledge and confidence to make informed money decisions. Financial literacy is a cornerstone of long-term financial well-being, yet too many people, especially young people, still lack access to trustworthy, practical financial education. Schwab’s commitment is rooted in the belief that when people have the skills to make informed decisions about their money, they can create greater stability for themselves, their families, and their communities.
